one_time_description: Robinson Huron Treaty past compensation settlement received from Federal and Ontario Provincial Government for past compensation obligations under the Robinson Huron Treaty of 1850
economic_notes: First Nation operates Tim Hortons and Esso business operations. Has investments in Shwe Miikaan Corp (33.333% ownership) and Shwe Miikaan Limited Partnership (33.333% ownership). Also has investments in Ontario First Nation Sovereign Wealth L.P. and OFN Asset Management GP Corp. Two inactive subsidiaries: Magnetawan Development GP Inc. and Magnetawan Development Limited Partnership.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED due to unfinalized financial statements of Shwe Miikaan Corp (SMC) and Shwe Miikaan Limited Partnership (SMLP) — auditors could not determine whether adjustments to revenue, surplus/deficiency, or cash flows were needed. Major one-time item: Robinson Huron Treaty settlement receivable of $220,866,577 recorded as financial asset; $220,245,577 recognized as revenue. Accumulated surplus jumped from ~$6.8M to ~$227M primarily due to this settlement. Net financial assets swung from net debt of ($5,068,457) to net assets of $214,811,298. Prior year comparative figures show deficiency of revenues over expenses of ($584,310). Two consolidated inactive subsidiaries included. SMLP investment increased significantly due to share of net earnings of $529,208 recognized (note: SMLP statements only finalized to Dec 31, 2022). Prior revenue field represents the deficiency amount rather than gross revenue as comparative gross revenue not separately extractable from the format shown.
